words.definitionEn: used to show that you are grateful to somebody for something they have done
words.exampleEn: ‘How are you?’ ‘Fine, thanks (= thanks for asking).’

words.definitionEn: words or actions that show that you are grateful to somebody for something
words.exampleEn: thanks to somebody for something, How can I ever express my thanks to you for all you've done?

words.definitionEn: used after some verbs, adjectives and nouns to introduce a new part of the sentence
words.exampleEn: She said (that) the story was true.

words.definitionEn: used for referring to a person or thing that is not near the speaker or as near to the speaker as another
words.exampleEn: Look at that man over there.

words.definitionEn: used for referring to a person or thing that is not near the speaker, or not as near to the speaker as another
words.exampleEn: Who's that?

words.definitionEn: used to refer to somebody/something that has already been mentioned or is easily understood
words.exampleEn: There were three questions. The first two were relatively easy but the third one was hard.

words.definitionEn: a building or an outdoor area where plays and similar types of entertainment are performed
words.exampleEn: Broadway theatres

words.definitionEn: in or into the space separating two or more points, objects, people, etc.
words.exampleEn: Q comes between P and R in the English alphabet.

words.definitionEn: of or belonging to people, animals or things that have already been mentioned or are easily identified
words.exampleEn: Their parties are always fun.

words.definitionEn: used when referring to people, animals or things as the object of a verb or preposition, or after the verb be
words.exampleEn: Tell them the news.

words.definitionEn: used to refer to a particular time in the past or future
words.exampleEn: Life was harder then because neither of us had a job.

words.definitionEn: used to show that something exists or happens
words.exampleEn: There's a restaurant around the corner.

words.definitionEn: people, animals or things that have already been mentioned or are easily identified
words.exampleEn: ‘Where are John and Liz?’ ‘They went for a walk.’

words.definitionEn: an object whose name you do not use because you do not need to or want to, or because you do not know it
words.exampleEn: Can you pass me that thing over there?

words.definitionEn: to have a particular idea or opinion about something/somebody; to believe something
words.exampleEn: think (that)…, Do you really think (that) he'll win?

words.definitionEn: 3rd
words.exampleEn: Today is the third (of May).

words.definitionEn: needing or wanting to drink
words.exampleEn: We were hungry and thirsty.

words.definitionEn: used to refer to a particular person, thing or event that is close to you, especially compared with another
words.exampleEn: How long have you been living in this country?

words.definitionEn: a road vehicle with two wheels that you ride by pushing the pedals with your feet
words.exampleEn: He got on his bicycle and rode off.
There were thousands of people there.
There are only three cookies left.

words.definitionEn: from one end or side of something to the other
words.exampleEn: Put the coffee in the filter and let the water run through.

words.definitionEn: from one end or side of something/somebody to the other
words.exampleEn: The burglar got in through the window.

words.definitionEn: the day of the week after Wednesday and before Friday
words.exampleEn: It's Thursday today, isn't it?

words.definitionEn: a printed piece of paper, or a message or image received on your phone or computer, that gives you the right to travel on a particular bus, train, etc. or to go into a theatre, etc.
words.exampleEn: a plane/bus/train ticket

words.definitionEn: what is measured in minutes, hours, days, etc.
words.exampleEn: The world exists in time and space.

words.definitionEn: large in size, degree, amount, etc.
words.exampleEn: a big man/house/increase

words.definitionEn: feeling that you would like to sleep or rest; needing rest
words.exampleEn: to be/look/feel tired

words.definitionEn: the name of a book, poem, painting, piece of music, etc.
words.exampleEn: What's title of her new book?

words.definitionEn: a bicycle
words.exampleEn: I used to ride my bike around the neighbourhood for hours.

words.definitionEn: used to show purpose or intention
words.exampleEn: I set out to buy food.

words.definitionEn: in the direction of something; towards something
words.exampleEn: I walked to the office.

words.definitionEn: on this day
words.exampleEn: I've got a piano lesson later today.

words.definitionEn: this day
words.exampleEn: Today is her tenth birthday.

words.definitionEn: with or near to somebody/something else; with each other
words.exampleEn: We grew up together.

words.definitionEn: a large bowl attached to a pipe that you sit on or stand over when you get rid of waste matter from your body
words.exampleEn: Have you flushed the toilet?

words.definitionEn: a document that shows how much you owe somebody for goods or services
words.exampleEn: the phone/electricity/gas bill

words.definitionEn: a soft fruit with a lot of juice and shiny red skin that is eaten as a vegetable either raw or cooked
words.exampleEn: a bacon, lettuce, and tomato sandwich

words.definitionEn: on or during the day after today
words.exampleEn: I'm off now. See you tomorrow.

words.definitionEn: the day after today
words.exampleEn: Today is Tuesday, so tomorrow is Wednesday.

words.definitionEn: on or during the evening or night of today
words.exampleEn: Will you have dinner with me tonight?

words.definitionEn: the evening or night of today
words.exampleEn: Here are tonight's football results.

words.definitionEn: used before adjectives and adverbs to say that something is more than is good, necessary, possible, etc.
words.exampleEn: He's far too young to go on his own.

words.definitionEn: any of the hard white structures in the mouth used for biting food
words.exampleEn: I've just had a tooth out at the dentist's.

words.definitionEn: a subject that you talk, write or learn about
words.exampleEn: The main topic of conversation was Tom's new girlfriend.

words.definitionEn: a person who is travelling or visiting a place for pleasure
words.exampleEn: busloads of foreign tourists

words.definitionEn: a place with many houses, shops, etc. where people live and work. It is larger than a village but smaller than a city.
words.exampleEn: The nearest town is ten miles away.

words.definitionEn: the vehicles that are on a road at a particular time
words.exampleEn: There's always a lot of traffic at this time of day.

words.definitionEn: a number of connected coaches or trucks, pulled by an engine or powered by a motor in each one, taking people and goods from one place to another
words.exampleEn: to get on/off a train

words.definitionEn: a creature that is covered with feathers and has two wings and two legs. Most birds can fly.
words.exampleEn: a bird’s nest with two eggs in it

words.definitionEn: the act or activity of travelling
words.exampleEn: air/rail/space travel

words.definitionEn: to go from one place to another, especially over a long distance
words.exampleEn: I go to bed early if I'm travelling the next day.

words.definitionEn: a tall plant that can live a long time. Trees have a thick central wooden trunk from which branches grow, usually with leaves on them
words.exampleEn: an oak/olive/apple tree

words.definitionEn: the day in each year which is the same date as the one on which you were born
words.exampleEn: Happy Birthday!

words.definitionEn: a journey to a place and back again, especially a short one for pleasure or a particular purpose
words.exampleEn: Did you have a good trip?

words.definitionEn: a piece of clothing that covers the lower body and is divided into two parts to cover each leg separately
words.exampleEn: a pair of grey trousers

words.definitionEn: connected with facts rather than things that have been invented or guessed
words.exampleEn: Indicate whether the following statements are true or false.

words.definitionEn: to make an attempt or effort to do or get something
words.exampleEn: I don't know if I can come but I'll try.

words.definitionEn: the day of the week after Monday and before Wednesday
words.exampleEn: It's Tuesday today, isn't it?

words.definitionEn: the time when somebody in a group of people should or is allowed to do something
words.exampleEn: When it's your turn, take another card.

words.definitionEn: to move or make something move around a central point
words.exampleEn: The wheels of the car began to turn.

words.definitionEn: television
words.exampleEn: We spent the evening watching TV.
There are only twelve of these rare animals left.

words.definitionEn: two times; on two occasions
words.exampleEn: I don't know him well; I've only met him twice.
There are only two cookies left.

words.definitionEn: a class or group of people or things that share particular qualities or features and are part of a larger group; a kind or sort
words.exampleEn: a rare blood type

words.definitionEn: an object with a round folding frame of long, straight pieces of metal covered with material, that you use to protect yourself from the rain or from hot sun
words.exampleEn: to carry/hold an umbrella

words.definitionEn: the brother of your mother or father; the husband of your aunt or uncle
words.exampleEn: Uncle Ian

words.definitionEn: below something
words.exampleEn: He pulled up the covers and crawled under.

words.definitionEn: in, to or through a position that is below something
words.exampleEn: Have you looked under the bed?

words.definitionEn: to know or realize the meaning of words, a language, what somebody says, etc.
words.exampleEn: understand (something), Can you understand French?

words.definitionEn: having the very darkest colour, like coal or the sky at night
words.exampleEn: a shiny black car

words.definitionEn: an institution at the highest level of education where you can study for a degree or do research
words.exampleEn: Is there a university in this town?

words.definitionEn: the very darkest colour, like night or coal
words.exampleEn: the black of the night sky

words.definitionEn: up to the point in time or the event mentioned
words.exampleEn: Let's wait until the rain stops.

words.definitionEn: up to the point in time or the event mentioned
words.exampleEn: Let's wait until the rain stops.

words.definitionEn: towards or in a higher position
words.exampleEn: He jumped up from his chair.

words.definitionEn: to or in a higher position somewhere
words.exampleEn: She climbed up the flight of steps.

words.definitionEn: up the stairs; on or to a floor of a house or other building higher than the one that you are on
words.exampleEn: to run/walk/head upstairs

words.definitionEn: used when the speaker or writer and another or others are the object of a verb or preposition, or after the verb be
words.exampleEn: She gave us a picture as a wedding present.

words.definitionEn: to do something with a machine, a method, an object, etc. for a particular purpose
words.exampleEn: use something, Can I use the photocopier?

words.definitionEn: that can help you to do or achieve what you want
words.exampleEn: a useful tool

words.definitionEn: in the way that is usual or normal; most often
words.exampleEn: How long does the journey usually take?

words.definitionEn: a period of time spent travelling or resting away from home
words.exampleEn: on vacation, They're on vacation in Hawaii right now.

words.definitionEn: a plant or part of a plant that is eaten as food. Potatoes, beans and onions are all vegetables.
words.exampleEn: The children don't eat enough fresh vegetables.

words.definitionEn: used before adjectives, adverbs and determiners to mean ‘in a high degree’ or ‘extremely’
words.exampleEn: Very few people know that.

words.definitionEn: a system of recording moving pictures and sound, either using a digital method of storing data or (in the past) using videotape
words.exampleEn: A wedding is the perfect subject for video.

words.definitionEn: a very small town located in a country area
words.exampleEn: We visited towns and villages all over Spain.

words.definitionEn: an occasion or a period of time when somebody goes to see a place or person and spends time there
words.exampleEn: a two-day/three-day visit

words.definitionEn: to go to see a person or a place for a period of time
words.exampleEn: My parents are coming to visit me next week.

words.definitionEn: a person who visits a person or place
words.exampleEn: We've got visitors coming this weekend.

words.definitionEn: to stay where you are or delay doing something until somebody/something comes or something happens
words.exampleEn: She rang the bell and waited.

words.definitionEn: a man whose job is to serve customers at their tables in a restaurant, etc.
words.exampleEn: I'll ask the waiter for the bill.

words.definitionEn: to stop sleeping; to make somebody stop sleeping
words.exampleEn: I always wake early in the summer.

words.definitionEn: a journey on foot, usually for pleasure or exercise
words.exampleEn: Let's go for a walk.

words.definitionEn: to move or go somewhere by putting one foot in front of the other on the ground, but without running
words.exampleEn: The baby is just learning to walk.

words.definitionEn: a long, solid structure that rises straight up from the ground, made of stone, brick or concrete, that surrounds, divides or protects an area of land
words.exampleEn: to build a wall

words.definitionEn: to have a desire or a wish for something/somebody
words.exampleEn: want somebody/something, Do you want some more tea?

words.definitionEn: at a fairly high temperature in a way that is pleasant, rather than being hot or cold
words.exampleEn: warm weather/temperatures/air

words.definitionEn: to make something/somebody clean using water and usually soap
words.exampleEn: wash something/somebody, These jeans need washing.

words.definitionEn: a type of small clock that you wear on your wrist, or (in the past) carried in your pocket
words.exampleEn: She kept looking anxiously at her watch.

words.definitionEn: to look at somebody/something for a time, paying attention to what happens
words.exampleEn: watch somebody/something, I was in the living room, watching TV.

words.definitionEn: a liquid without colour, smell or taste that falls as rain, is in lakes, rivers and seas, and is used for drinking, washing, etc.
words.exampleEn: a glass of water

words.definitionEn: a method, style or manner of doing something
words.exampleEn: I prefer to do things the easy way.

words.definitionEn: I and another person or other people; I and you
words.exampleEn: We've moved to Atlanta.

words.definitionEn: to have something on your body as a piece of clothing, a decoration, etc.
words.exampleEn: He was wearing a new suit.

words.definitionEn: the condition of the atmosphere at a particular place and time, such as the temperature, and if there is wind, rain, sun, etc.
words.exampleEn: cold/hot/warm/wet/dry weather

words.definitionEn: a set of pages on the internet, where a company or an organization, or an individual person, puts information
words.exampleEn: For current prices please visit our website.

words.definitionEn: the day of the week after Tuesday and before Thursday
words.exampleEn: It's Wednesday today, isn't it?

words.definitionEn: a period of seven days, either from Monday to Sunday or from Sunday to Saturday
words.exampleEn: last/this/next week

words.definitionEn: Saturday and Sunday
words.exampleEn: this/next/last weekend

words.definitionEn: a website where an individual person, or people representing an organization, write regularly about recent events or topics that interest them, usually with photos and links to other websites that they find interesting
words.exampleEn: This is a link to the museum's blog.

words.definitionEn: accepted or wanted somewhere
words.exampleEn: I'll go, then. I know when I'm not welcome.

words.definitionEn: used as a greeting to tell somebody that you are pleased that they are there
words.exampleEn: Welcome home!

words.definitionEn: to say hello to somebody in a friendly way when they arrive somewhere
words.exampleEn: welcome somebody, They were at the door to welcome us.

words.definitionEn: in good health
words.exampleEn: I don't feel very well.

words.definitionEn: pale gold in colour
words.exampleEn: She had long blonde hair.

words.definitionEn: in a good, right or acceptable way
words.exampleEn: They played well in the tournament.

words.definitionEn: used to express surprise, anger or relief
words.exampleEn: Well, well—I would never have guessed it!

words.definitionEn: in or towards the west
words.exampleEn: West Africa

words.definitionEn: towards the west
words.exampleEn: This room faces west.

words.definitionEn: the direction that you look towards to see the sun go down; one of the four main points of the compass
words.exampleEn: Which way is west?

words.definitionEn: used in questions to ask for particular information about somebody/something
words.exampleEn: What is your name?

words.definitionEn: used in questions to ask for particular information about somebody/something
words.exampleEn: What is your name?

words.definitionEn: at what time; on what occasion
words.exampleEn: When did you last see him?

words.definitionEn: at or during the time that
words.exampleEn: I loved history when I was at school.

words.definitionEn: what/which time
words.exampleEn: Until when can you stay?

words.definitionEn: in or to what place or situation
words.exampleEn: Where do you live?

words.definitionEn: (in) the place or situation in which
words.exampleEn: This is where I live.

words.definitionEn: used in questions to ask somebody to be exact about one or more people or things from a limited number
words.exampleEn: Which is better exercise—swimming or tennis?

words.definitionEn: used in questions to ask somebody to be exact about one or more people or things from a limited number
words.exampleEn: Which is better exercise—swimming or tennis?
